origin.name: Senegal
origin.key_document: Acte de deces (death certificate from Centre d'etat civil, requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Senegal travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Dakar guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Senegal is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option. You will need the death certificate, cremation certificate, and export documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Acte de deces (certified English translation required)
special_rules: All French-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
Police clearance is required for all violent or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from the relevant Senegalese authority is required for all repatriations.
Embalming must be carried out by a licensed funeral director before air transport.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Senegalese documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Dakar can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Senegal to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Senegal. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-4 weeks. Etat civil registration, French translation, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
